heading: Establishment; notice in Federal Register; administration
label: 230e Establishment; notice in Federal Register; administration
text: The Secretary shall administer the park in accordance with the provisions of this part, the Act of (), the Act of (), and any other statutory authorities available to him for the conservation and management of natural, historical, and cultural resources.August 25, 1916August 21, 193539 Stat. 535149 Stat. 6661See References in Text note below.1
notes: References in TextThe Act of (), referred to in text, is , , known as the National Park Service Organic Act, which enacted sections 1, 2, 3, and 4 of this title and provisions set out as a note under , National Park Service and Related Programs. Sections 1 to 4 of the Act were repealed and restated as , Crimes and Criminal Procedure, and section 100101(a), chapter 1003, and sections 100751(a), 100752, 100753, and 102101 of Title 54 by , §§ 3, 4(a)(1), 7, , , 3260, 3272. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables. For disposition of former sections of this title, see Disposition Table preceding .August 25, 1916Dec. 19, 201439 Stat. 535act Aug. 25, 1916, ch. 40839 Stat. 535section 100101 of Title 54section 1865(a) of Title 18Pub. L. 113–287128 Stat. 3094section 100101 of Title 54The Act of (), referred to in text, is , , known as the Historic Sites Act of 1935 and also as the Historic Sites, Buildings, and Antiquities Act, which enacted sections 461 to 467 of this title. The Act was repealed and restated as , Crimes and Criminal Procedure, and sections 102303 and 102304 and chapter 3201 of Title 54, National Park Service and Related Programs, by , §§ 3, 4(a)(1), 7, , , 3260, 3272. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables. For disposition of former sections of this title, see Disposition Table preceding .August 21, 1935Dec. 19, 201449 Stat. 666act Aug. 21, 1935, ch. 59349 Stat. 666section 1866(a) of Title 18Pub. Amendments2009— substituted “The” for “The Secretary shall establish the park by publication of a notice to that effect in the Federal Register at such time as he finds that, consistent with the general management plan referred to in , sufficient lands and interests therein (i) have been acquired for interpretive and administrative facilities, (ii) are being protected in the core area, and (iii) have been made the subject of cooperative agreements pursuant to secton 230c of this title. Pending such establishment and thereafter the”.Pub. L. 111–11section 230g of this title
“Secretary” DefinedSecretary means the Secretary of the Interior, see , set out as a note under .section 2 of Pub. L. 95–625section 45f of this title
